meta.label: Nigerian Pepper Soup
meta.description: Nigerian pepper soup: a light, aromatic, spicy broth seasoned with pepper soup spice blend, traditionally made with goat meat or fish, and served hot with scent leaf or uziza.

source.author: Suka Smart Assistant
source.reference: Traditional Nigerian pepper soup profile with spice blend and scent leaf/uziza; standardized for SSA catalog.

summary: Aromatic Nigerian pepper soup with goat meat (or fish), built on pepper soup spice blend and fresh aromatics for a light but intensely flavorful, spicy broth served piping hot.

notes: Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free; confirm bouillon is GF if used.
For goat: simmer until tender; tougher cuts may need longer.
For fish: add near the end and avoid overboiling so it doesn’t break apart; remove bones before serving.

ingredients.label: Goat meat (bone-in pieces preferred)
notes: Classic choice. Substitute: chicken, beef, or fish (see variation).

ingredients.label: Pepper soup spice blend
notes: About 2 Tbsp, or to taste depending on blend strength.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet/habanero, whole or sliced
notes: Use whole for gentler heat; slice for hotter.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Add to taste; start lower if using bouillon.

ingredients.label: Scent leaf (efirin) or basil, chopped
notes: Add at the end for aroma.

ingredients.label: Uziza leaf (optional), chopped
notes: Optional; adds a peppery herbal note.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Adjust depending on pot size and desired strength.
Pressure cooker can reduce goat cook time; then finish herbs/spice on the stove.

steps.title: Rinse and prep goat meat
steps.body: Rinse goat pieces and trim any excess surface fat. Pat dry. Optional: parboil 5 minutes, drain, and rinse again if you want a cleaner broth.

steps.title: Build the broth and start simmering
steps.body: Add goat meat, water, onions, ginger, and garlic to a pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer. Skim any foam if desired. Cover partially and cook until goat is tender, 60–75 minutes (longer for tougher cuts).

steps.title: Season with pepper soup spices
steps.body: Stir in pepper soup spice blend, black pepper, scotch bonnet (whole or sliced), salt, and optional bouillon. Simmer 10 minutes to bloom spices and deepen aroma.

steps.title: Finish with herbs/greens
steps.body: Turn off heat. Stir in chopped scent leaf (or basil) and optional uziza. Cover 2 minutes to steep. Taste and adjust salt and heat.

steps.title: Serve hot
steps.body: Serve piping hot in bowls with meat. Optional accompaniments include boiled yam, rice, or bread. Refrigerate leftovers up to 4 days or freeze up to 2 months.

notes: Signature profile: pepper soup is light but intensely aromatic—pepper soup spice blend + fresh herbs are the core.
Heat control: add scotch bonnet whole for gentler heat; slice or crush for hotter broth.
Herb timing: add scent leaf/uziza at the end so the aroma stays bright.
Goat tenderness: if goat is tough, simmer longer or use a pressure cooker then finish with spices/herbs.

variations.label: Fish pepper soup
notes: Use 900 g firm fish (catfish/tilapia). Simmer aromatics 15 minutes first, then add fish and pepper soup spice; cook gently 10–12 minutes. Add herbs at the end and remove bones before serving.

variations.label: Chicken pepper soup
notes: Use 900 g bone-in chicken pieces. Simmer 30–40 minutes until tender, then proceed with spices and herbs.

variations.label: Extra spicy
notes: Increase pepper soup spice blend by 1–2 tsp and add a second scotch bonnet (or add a pinch of cayenne).
